subject: Re: CTF multiplying raw subtomograms from warp
body:
Hi Pranav,

Thanks for the link and the advice!! (I am not trying to do subtraction 
though, just an example :) )

Joe

On Wednesday, December 27, 2023 at 12:53:16 PM UTC+1 p.sha...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi Joe,
> The design of the 3d ctf model is explained here -
>
> https://www.cell.com/structure/fulltext/S0969-2126(15)00279-8?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS0969212615002798%3Fshowall%3Dtrue
>
> In STA it is not necessary to perform subtraction, simple masking is 
> sufficient.
>
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
> On Tue, 26 Dec 2023 at 18:08, joe dobbs <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi,
>>
>> Thanks for your message, that's very good to know! 
>>
>> Would you happen to know about any documentation that describes this? I 
>> wasn't able to find anything about it in the warp paper or original relion 
>> subtomo paper (unless I missed it!) and wrongly assumed that since ctf info 
>> is provided separately, and that some relion operations not implemented for 
>> subtomos (eg. signal subtraction) fail with an error message about images 
>> needing to be ctf-multipied, that the subtomos weren't already ctf 
>> multiplied.
>>
>> In any case, thanks for the help!
>>
>> On Tuesday, December 26, 2023 at 6:04:27 PM UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Joe,
>>> The CTF file is for multiplying an orient reference to best match the 
>>> observation(your subtomo). The discrepancy in the file sizes is due to the 
>>> symmetric nature of a fourier transform. If you are using warp generated 
>>> subtomos you needn’t have to multiply the two files together.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Pranav
>>> --
>>> Pranav Shah
>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>
>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>> University of Oxford,
>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>> UK
>>>
>>>
>>> On Tue, 26 Dec 2023 at 13:09, joe dobbs <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi all,
>>>>
>>>> I'm looking at CTF correcting my warp subtomograms directly. When 
>>>> trying to multiply the subtomos with their ctf files via imod clip, I found 
>>>> that these are actually different sizes (eg. subtomos are 96x96x96 vs. ctf 
>>>> 49x96x96), so I assume direct multiplication might not be the best way to 
>>>> do it. Would anyone have any suggestions about the proper way to do this?
>>>>
>>>> Thanks!
>>>> Joe
>>>>
>>>> -- 
>>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>>> Groups "Warp" group.

>>>> .
